facts.text: Detroit was founded in 1701 by explorers Antoine de la Mothe Cadillac and Alphonse de Tonty, who named the settlement Fort Pontchartrain du Détroit after a naval secretary.
Detroit founded Motown Records, which rose to prominence in the 1960s with stars like Stevie Wonder, Diana Ross, and the Jackson 5.
Detroit hosts three major sports arenas: Comerica Park for the Tigers, Ford Field for the Lions, and Little Caesars Arena for the Red Wings and Pistons.
Detroit has made seven bids to host the Summer Olympics, more than any other city, yet has never been awarded the games.
The Detroit Historical Society, founded in 1921, curates exhibits that trace the city’s evolution from a French fort to a modern metropolis.
